title: Fix subagent packet defects

tasks.title: Patch ADV sub-agent packet-defect prompt policy
tasks.verification: RED: pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-task-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts src/adv-engineer-assets.test.ts failed because top-level ADV lacked typed worker packet policy and leaf agents lacked packet_defect handling. GREEN: same command passed (3 files, 110 tests) after adding orchestrator-owned packet identity policy and leaf structured packet-defect failure instructions.

tasks.title: Run focused verification and full check
tasks.verification: Focused verification passed: pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-task-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts src/adv-engineer-assets.test.ts (3 files, 110 tests). Initial pnpm run check failed on Prettier formatting in two tests; ran pnpm run format. Rerun focused verification passed (110 tests). Final pnpm run check passed: typecheck, test-isolation, lockfile policy, lint, format:check.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal/plan with reply `approve` after repeated repro; proposal includes success criteria and scope.

discovery.approval_evidence: Agreement artifact persisted. User approved with reply `approve`. Acceptance criteria AC1-AC5, constraints C1-C3, avoidances DONT1-DONT2.

design.approval_evidence: Design is small instruction-contract hardening. No product tradeoff or validator conflict. Scope limited to ADV prompts/tests; no delegation routing redesign.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved implementation plan with reply `approve`. Task graph: prompt policy patch + focused/full verification. adv_change_validate strict passed with non-blocking NO_DELTAS warning.

execution.approval_evidence: Both tasks completed with checkpoints. Focused asset tests passed (3 files, 110 tests). pnpm run check passed after formatting-only remediation. adv_change_validate strict passed with non-blocking NO_DELTAS warning.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User approved acceptance with reply `approve`. Contract review matrix has 10 rows, 0 failing. Focused tests and pnpm run check passed. Executive summary persisted.

items.text: Top-level ADV instructions state typed worker spawns must include `WORKING DIRECTORY`, `CHANGE`, `TASK`, and `ATTEMPT`; `adv-reviewer` typed workers must also include `PHASE`.
Top-level ADV instructions state missing typed-worker packet identity fields are orchestrator defects handled internally by corrected retry or inline fallback, never by user `question`.

`adv-reviewer` and `adv-engineer` prompts do not instruct leaf workers to ask the user/orchestrator via `question` for missing `TASK`, `PHASE`, `ATTEMPT`, or `WORKING DIRECTORY`; they return structured packet-defect failure for orchestrator recovery.
Asset tests fail if user-facing `question` wording returns for missing packet identity fields.
Focused asset tests and `pnpm run check` pass.
Do not add persisted report support for `adv-researcher` or `adv-tron`.
Do not make users provide `TASK`, `PHASE`, `ATTEMPT`, `sessionID`, or other packet identity values.
Do not rely on prose-only guidance without tests.

rows.evidence: Passed: src/adv-task-assets.test.ts asserts top-level ADV contains Typed worker packet contract with WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, TASK, ATTEMPT, adv-reviewer PHASE, orchestrator-owned, never ask user, corrected retry, inline fallback.
Passed: src/adv-task-assets.test.ts asserts missing typed-worker packet fields are orchestrator-owned and handled by retry with corrected packet or continue inline, never ask user.

Passed: src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts and src/adv-engineer-assets.test.ts assert missing packet identity fields produce packet_defect structured failure and forbid question/ask-orchestrator wording in relevant sections.
RED evidence: focused tests failed before prompt changes because policy/packet_defect wording was absent. GREEN evidence: same tests passed after patch.
Passed: pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-task-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts src/adv-engineer-assets.test.ts (3 files, 110 tests). Passed: pnpm run check.
No changes to plugin/src/types/subagent-reports.ts or adv_subagent_report_submit schemas.
No persisted report schema/support added for adv-researcher or adv-tron.
Changes limited to ADV prompt policy and asset tests; no delegation routing code changed.
Prompts now explicitly say packet identity values are not user questions; top-level says never ask user for them.
Prompt guidance backed by asset tests in adv-task-assets, adv-reviewer-asset, and adv-engineer-assets.

acceptanceCriteria: Top-level ADV instructions state typed worker spawns must include `WORKING DIRECTORY`, `CHANGE`, `TASK`, and `ATTEMPT`; `adv-reviewer` typed workers must also include `PHASE`.
Top-level ADV instructions state missing typed-worker packet identity fields are orchestrator defects handled internally by corrected retry or inline fallback, never by user `question`.

`adv-reviewer` and `adv-engineer` prompts do not instruct leaf workers to ask the user/orchestrator via `question` for missing `TASK`, `PHASE`, `ATTEMPT`, or `WORKING DIRECTORY`; they return structured packet-defect failure for orchestrator recovery.
Asset tests fail if user-facing `question` wording returns for missing packet identity fields.
Focused asset tests and `pnpm run check` pass.
